task: Write a Python function trimmed_mean(nums) returning the mean of the list after dropping exactly one occurrence of the minimum and one occurrence of the maximum, as a float. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided Python function correctly calculates the trimmed mean by removing one occurrence of the minimum and maximum values from the input list.
trace: To handle the task, I first considered the edge case of an empty or non-list input by returning `None`. Then, for the main function, I removed the minimum and maximum values from the list once each while ensuring that the list is not empty. Lastly, I calculated the mean by summing the remaining elements in the list and dividing it by the new length of the list. This method ensures concise and readable code while accounting for the named edge case.
